# cognito-toolkit (v2)

> Zero-runtime-dependency, ESM-only micro-library that validates AWS Cognito JWTs (id / access tokens) for authentication & authorization. `makeGetUser(pools, options?)` returns an async validator that fetches the pool's JWKS, verifies the signature under a configurable algorithm policy (default `['RS256']`), checks issuer / `kid` / `exp` / `nbf` plus an optional `validate` hook, and resolves to the decoded payload or `null`. Works against non-Cognito OIDC issuers too. Two `utils/` helpers fetch and cache OAuth2 `client_credentials` access tokens. Built on Node built-ins (`crypto`, `fetch`, `util.debuglog`) — no AWS SDK. Runtime targets Node 20+, latest Bun, latest Deno.

## Install

```sh
npm install cognito-toolkit
```

## Quick start

```js
import makeGetUser from 'cognito-toolkit';

const getUser = makeGetUser({region: 'us-east-1', userPoolId: 'us-east-1_AbCdef'});

// per request: pass a JWT taken from a header or cookie
const user = await getUser(authHeader || authCookie);
if (user) {
  // `user` is the decoded, verified JWT payload
} else {
  // not authenticated
}
```

CommonJS consumers use the named export: `const {makeGetUser} = require('cognito-toolkit')`.

## Sub-exports

- `cognito-toolkit` — `makeGetUser` (default + named) + `SUPPORTED_ALGORITHMS`. Builds a token validator for one or more Cognito user pools (or any OIDC issuer).
- `cognito-toolkit/utils/lazy-access-token` — `createLazyAccessToken` (default + named). On-demand, cached `client_credentials` access-token holder.
- `cognito-toolkit/utils/renewable-access-token` — `createRenewableAccessToken` (default + named). Timer-renewed `client_credentials` access-token holder.

## API

### `makeGetUser(pools, globalOptions?) -> (token) => Promise<payload | null>`

`pools` is a pool object or an array of them:

- `region` — AWS region, e.g. `'us-east-1'`. Required unless `issuer` is given.
- `userPoolId` — user pool id, e.g. `'us-east-1_AbCdef'`. Required unless `issuer` is given.
- `issuer` — full issuer URL, overriding `region` + `userPoolId` (non-Cognito OIDC or testing). JWKS is read from `${issuer}/.well-known/jwks.json`.

`globalOptions` (optional):

- `algorithms` — allowed signing algorithms: a JWA-name array or a predicate `(alg, header) => boolean`. Default: `['RS256']`. Verifiable set = `SUPPORTED_ALGORITHMS` (`RS256/384/512`, `PS256/384/512`, `ES256/384/512`); `HS*` and `none` are always rejected, so widening the list cannot enable algorithm confusion.
- `validate` — optional `(payload, header) => boolean | Promise<boolean>` gate run after signature + `exp` / `nbf`. Falsy or throw → reject. For claims like `token_use`, `aud`, `client_id`.
- `fetch` — custom `fetch` implementation. Default: global `fetch`.
- `minRefreshInterval` — minimum ms between JWKS refreshes triggered by an unknown `kid` (rotation handling). Default: `0`.
- `clockTolerance` — allowed clock skew in seconds for `exp` / `nbf`. Default: `0`.

The returned validator resolves to the decoded payload when the algorithm policy, signature, issuer, `kid`, `exp` / `nbf`, and `validate` hook all check out; otherwise `null`. It never throws on malformed input. JWKS keys are fetched lazily and refreshed automatically on key rotation. The token header's `alg` is only checked against the `algorithms` policy — never used to choose how the token is verified.

### Other OIDC providers

Pass `issuer` instead of `region` + `userPoolId` and set `algorithms` to what the provider signs with:

```js
const getUser = makeGetUser({issuer: 'https://accounts.example.com'}, {algorithms: ['ES256'], validate: p => p.aud === 'my-api'});
```

### `createLazyAccessToken({url, clientId, secret, fetch?}) -> {authorize, getToken}`

`authorize()` returns a cached unexpired token or fetches a fresh one; `getToken()` returns the current token without fetching. State is per-instance.

### `createRenewableAccessToken({url, clientId, secret, fetch?}) -> {retrieveToken, cancelRenewal, getToken}`

`retrieveToken()` fetches a token and schedules an automatic refresh shortly before expiry (the timer is `unref`ed). `cancelRenewal(clearToken?)` stops the refresh. Always read the live token via `getToken()`.

## Notes

- Zero runtime dependencies; verification is offline except the public JWKS fetch.
- ESM-only with hand-written `.d.ts` sidecars; no build step.
- v2 is a breaking rewrite of v1 (CommonJS, `jsonwebtoken` + `jwk-to-pem`). The `makeGetUser` contract is unchanged; the `utils/` are now factories instead of module-level singletons.
